title: Every 'My' Is A Lie Your Ego Tells
hook: Count how many times you say 'my' today. That's how often you're lying to yourself.

script.visual: Quick cuts of hand gestures: pointing to a laptop, a phone, a reflection in a mirror, a chest. Each labeled with 'MY' in hard white type.
script.voiceover: Count how many times you say 'my' today. That's how often you're lying to yourself.

script.visual: Person at a desk. Phone buzzes. They glance at it, shoulders tighten. Text overlays pop in rapid succession next to them: 'my email,' 'my calendar,' 'my anxiety,' 'my deadline.'
script.voiceover: My job. My reputation. My anxiety. My body. We glue our identity onto things a hundred times a day and never notice we're doing it. Shankara had a word for this — Adhyasa. Superimposition. Gluing 'I' onto what you are not.

script.visual: Close-up: person slowly peeling a sticker off a glass surface. The sticker says 'ME'. Underneath: clear glass. They hold the sticker up to the light.
script.voiceover: Here's why it matters. When your job fails, 'you' feel destroyed. When someone criticizes your body, 'you' feel attacked. Not because the job or the body is actually you — but because you stuck a label on them and forgot the label wasn't the thing. Shankara says the wise person peels the labels off one by one.

script.visual: Person sitting still, eyes soft. Voice drops to intimate register. Camera slow push-in.
script.voiceover: Try this right now. In your head, say 'my anxiety.' Notice: there is the anxiety, and there is the one aware of it. Those are not the same. The one who notices can't be what's being noticed. Every time you catch a 'my,' you get a door. The door asks: is this really me, or is this something I'm aware of?
script.text_overlay: Is this me? Or something I'm AWARE of?

script.visual: Hands open, empty. Person looks up, calm, almost amused.
script.voiceover: You are not your job. You are not your anxiety. You are not your body. You're the quiet awareness that's been aware of all of them, without ever becoming any of them.
script.text_overlay: You are what notices. Not what's noticed.

script.visual: Black card with one line of text.
script.voiceover: Which 'my' are you holding onto hardest right now?
script.text_overlay: Which 'my' hurts to let go of?

cta: Which 'my' hurts the most to question? Drop it in the comments. No judgment. Just honesty.
caption:
Count the 'my's in your internal monologue today.
That's how often your ego is writing a check your awareness can't cash.

Shankara called it Adhyasa — superimposition. Gluing your identity onto things that are not you. My job. My reputation. My anxiety. Every 'my' is a little knot the ego ties between 'I' and something that isn't you.

Verse 269: "The notion of 'I' and 'mine' in the body, senses, and other non-Self objects is superimposition. The wise person must remove this through abiding in the Self."

You don't have to burn your life down. You just have to notice: is this me, or is this something I'm aware of?

Those are not the same. That difference is the whole door.

अहंममेति यो भावो देहाक्षादावनात्मनि।
अध्यासोऽयं निरस्तव्यो विदुषा स्वात्मनिष्ठया॥ २६९॥

#vivekachudamani #vedanta #advaita #adhyasa #ego #shankara #selfinquiry #ancientwisdom #hinduphilosophy #consciousness #atman #nondualism #spirituality #awakening #identity
music_suggestion: Minimal piano — single notes with space between them, like the labels peeling off. Builds to a subtle drone in the 25-38s inquiry section. Silence for the final line.
